titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 566

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=874 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.57781+00 | translation moa=875 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.58958+00

p.cl:
DD Rex scotorum Episcopis Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis . vicecomitibus . et Omnibus
probis hominibus suis scottis et anglis tocius terre sue Salutem .

Sciant presentes
et futuri me concessisse et hac carta mea confirmasse Diuisas inter Colding
ham et bonekil quas ego cum probis hominibus meis perambulare feci in perpetu
um tenendas libere et quiete plenarie . Scilicet a midlesdene heued per merebur
nes heued uersus occidentem usque ad crachoctre et Inde per eandem stratam usque ad Eifo
rd .

Testibus his . Iohanne Glascuensi episcopo hereberto cancellario . R . de brus . Paga
no de brausa . Hugone de more villa Berengario engain
David king of the Scots to his bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, and all his worthy men, Gael and English, of his entire land, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have conceded and by this my charter to have made firm the boundaries between Coldingham and Bunkle which I with my worthy men perambulated, to be held freely, quitly, and fully, in perpetuity. That is to say, from Milldown head westwards by Mereburn head as far as Crachotre, and from there by the same way as far as Eiford.
With these witnesses: John, bishop of Glasgow; Herbert the chancellor; Robert de Brus; Payn de Briouze; Hugh de Morville; Berengar Engaine.
